summary: Crystal‑clear Miller soundboard captures a high‑energy 1978 show highlighted by a soaring “Stella By Starlight” and monster jams on The Other One and Estimated Prophet.
review: The recording is a meticulously cleaned Miller SBD that sounds crisp, balanced and virtually free of hiss – the kind of soundboard tape fans call ‘godly’. Musically the concert shines with Jerry’s emotive vocals on Stella By Starlight, a ferocious The Other One > Stella sequence, an inspired Estimated Prophet jam, plus standout versions of Stagger Lee, Looks Like Rain and I Need a Miracle. Overall reviewers rave about the band’s tight pocket, dynamic transitions and the venue’s great acoustics.

ai_rating.rationale: Consistently high praise for both performance (standout songs, tight playing) and audio quality (crystal‑clear Miller SBD). Weighting gives a rating well above 4.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Miller SBD cleaned, repitched and patched; described as crystal clear, crisp, with only a minor glitch at LLR 6:58 noted by one reviewer.

standout_songs: The Other One > Stella By Starlight
show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑energy first set with tight transitions (Promised Land → Sugaree) and an even stronger second set featuring long, flowing jams; reviewers note the band stayed in the pocket throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Emotive vocal performance on Stella By Starlight; great fills and perfect tone noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Leads an excellent transition (Promised Land → Sugaree) and provides strong bass foundation.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey Hart & Bill Kreutzmann described as ‘in the pocket’; drum segment highlighted with guest Lee Oskar and a massive Gong.
